tbox.st: State Agency Fees and Internal Service Fund Authorization and Appropriations

lt.gd: This bill supplements or reduces appropriations otherwise provided for the support and operation of state government for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025 and ending June 30, 2026 and appropriates funds for the support and operation of state government for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2026 and ending June 30, 2027.
lt.hp: This bill:provides budget increases and decreases for the use and support of certain state agencies and institutions of higher education;authorizes certain state agency fees;authorizes internal service fund rates;adjusts funding for the impact of Internal Service Fund rate changes; and,provides budget increases and decreases for other purposes as described.
lt.moni: Money Appropriated in this Bill:This bill appropriates $3,754,700 in operating and capital budgets for fiscal year 2026, including:($1,471,200) from General Fund; and$411,700 from Income Tax Fund; and$4,814,200 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $33,300 in expendable funds and accounts for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $23,000 in business-like activities for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $7,200 in fiduciary funds for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $14,201,500 in operating and capital budgets for fiscal year 2027, including:$4,148,700 from General Fund; and$1,364,700 from Income Tax Fund; and$8,688,100 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $142,700 in expendable funds and accounts for fiscal year 2027, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $55,500 in business-like activities for fiscal year 2027, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $10,200 in fiduciary funds for fiscal year 2027, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.
lt.oc: Other Special Clauses:This bill provides a special effective date.

leg.enact: Be it enacted by the Legislature of the state of Utah:

sectionText.summary: The following sums of money are appropriated for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025, and ending June 30, 2026. These are additions to amounts previously appropriated for fiscal year 2026.

licat.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Title 63J, Chapter 1, Budgetary Procedures Act, the Legislature appropriates the following sums of money from the funds or accounts indicated for the use and support of the government of the state of Utah.

sectionText.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Utah Code Title 63J Chapter 1 and other fee statutes as applicable, the following fees and rates are approved for the use and support of the government of the State of Utah for the Fiscal Year beginning July 01, 2026 and ending June 30, 2027.

feeagency.feedisplay: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES

category.feedisplay: All the fees in this section apply for the entire Department of Health and Human Services
Government Records Access and Management Act
Staff time for file search and/or information compilation

fee.explanation: For Department of Technology Services or programmer/analyst staff time.
First 15 minutes free, additional time.

fee.feedisplay: Expert Testimony Fee for those without a PhD (Doctor of Philosophy) or MD (Medical Doctor)
fee.explanation: Includes preparation, consultation, and appearance on criminal and civil cases. Portal to portal, including travel and waiting time plus travel costs.

fee.feedisplay: Expert Testimony Fee for those with a PhD (Doctor of Philosophy) or MD (Medical Doctor)
fee.explanation: Includes preparation, consultation, and appearance on criminal and civil cases. Portal to portal, including travel and waiting time plus travel costs.
2-day shipping of online ordered vital records certificates
This allows the unmarried parents, who have since the birth gotten married, to change the information on the record to reflect married parents.

category.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Limited Data Series

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Limited Data Series - Fee Discounts
fee.explanation: Note: The following discounts apply: (1) Utah State agencies and Local Health Departments; components of the Indian health system serving Utah residents i.e. Indian Tribes, Indian Health Service, tribally owned and operated health systems, the Urban Indian Organization, and Tribal Epidemiology Centers (100%); Student (75% for any standard data set); Contributing Carrier (50% for standard limited use data sets); Geographic Subset (discount proportional to percent of records required from limited use data set, in addition to custom data services fee).

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Limited Data Set - Single Year
fee.explanation: This fee is for an all-payer claims database limited data set that includes one year specified by the requester. The data set contains 16 out of the 18 Safe Harbor identifiers related to a member's medical, pharmacy, and dental claims as well as insurance enrollment and health care provider data. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee prior to distribution of data to the user.

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Limited Data Set - Two Years
fee.explanation: This fee is for an all-payer claims database limited data set that includes two years specified by the requester. The data set contains 16 out of the 18 Safe Harbor identifiers related to a member's medical, pharmacy, and dental claims as well as insurance enrollment and health care provider data. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee prior to distribution of data to the user.

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Limited Data Set - Three Years
fee.explanation: This fee is for an all-payer claims database limited data set that includes three years specified by the requester. The data set contains 16 out of the 18 Safe Harbor identifiers related to a member's medical, pharmacy, and dental claims as well as insurance enrollment and health care provider data. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee prior to distribution of data to the user.

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Limited Data Set - Additional Years
fee.explanation: This fee is for an all-payer claims database limited data set that includes additional years specified by the requester. The data set contains 16 out of the 18 Safe Harbor identifiers related to a member's medical, pharmacy, and dental claims as well as insurance enrollment and health care provider data. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee prior to distribution of data to the user.

category.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Research Data Series

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Research Data Set - Fee Discounts
fee.explanation: Notes: The following discounts apply: Utah State agencies and Local Health Departments; components of the Indian health system serving Utah residents i.e. Indian Tribes, Indian Health Service, tribally owned and operated health systems, the Urban Indian Organization, and Tribal Epidemiology Centers (100%); Student (75% for any standard data set); Contributing Carrier (50% for standard limited use data sets); Geographic Subset (discount proportional to percent of records required from limited use data set, in addition to custom data services fee).

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Research Data Set - Single Year
fee.explanation: This fee is for an all-payer claims database identifiable data set that includes one year specified by the requester. The data set contains the 18 Safe Harbor identifiers related to medical, pharmacy, and dental claims as well as insurance enrollment and health care provider data. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee. The data set includes sensitive and detailed patient data.

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Research Data Set - Two Years
fee.explanation: This fee is for an all-payer claims database identifiable data set that includes two years specified by the requester. The data set contains the 18 Safe Harbor identifiers related to medical, pharmacy, and dental claims as well as insurance enrollment and health care provider data. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee. The data set includes sensitive and detailed patient data.

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Research Data Set - Three Years
fee.explanation: This fee is for an all-payer claims database identifiable data set that includes three years specified by the requester. The data set contains the 18 Safe Harbor identifiers related to medical, pharmacy, and dental claims as well as insurance enrollment and health care provider data. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee. The data set includes sensitive and detailed patient data.

fee.feedisplay: All Payer Claims Data Standard Research Data Set - Additional Years
fee.explanation: This fee is for an all-payer claims database limited data set that includes years specified by the requester. The data set contains 16 out of the 18 Safe Harbor identifiers related to a member's medical, pharmacy, and dental claims as well as insurance enrollment and health care provider data. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee prior to distribution of data to the user.

The Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System dataset provides surveillance data on health-related risk behaviors, chronic health conditions, and use of preventive services of the Utah population. The data is used for program planning, assessment work, evaluation projects, and research purposes. The following discounts apply: Local Health Department (100% for any standard annual data set); State Agency, Student or Not for Profit Entity (75% for any standard annual data set); Researcher (50% for any standard annual data set); For Profit Entities pay full amount. Note that entities that have paid to have questions included on the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System are excluded from this fee as their payment includes receipt of data.

fee.feedisplay: Healthcare Facilities Data Set - Fee Discounts
fee.explanation: Notes: (1) The Following Discounts Apply: Utah State agencies and Local Health Departments; and components of the Indian health system serving Utah residents i.e. Indian Tribes, Indian Health Service, tribally owned and operated health systems, the Urban Indian Organization, and Tribal Epidemiology Centers (100%); Student (75% for any standard data set); Utah Healthcare Facility with <35,000 discharges (50% for Standard Limited Data Set); Prior Years (25% for any data set); University or Not for Profit Entity (50% for any standard data series); Geographic Subset (discount proportional to percent of records required from limited use data set, including custom data services fee).

fee.feedisplay: Healthcare Facilities Data Set - Standard Annual Limited Data Set
fee.explanation: This fee allows a user to obtain a single year of inpatient or ambulatory surgery or emergency department encounter limited data set. It contains 16 out of the 18 safe harbor identifiers related to information about patient, diagnoses, provider, payers, charges and more. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee prior to distribution of data to the user.

fee.feedisplay: Healthcare Facilities Data Set - Standard Annual Research Data set
fee.explanation: This fee is for an all-payer claims database identifiable data set that includes a single year of data. The data set contains the 18 Safe Harbor identifiers related to medical, pharmacy, and dental claims as well as insurance enrollment and health care provider data. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee. The data set includes sensitive and detailed patient data.

fee.feedisplay: Healthcare Facilities Data Set - Quarterly Preliminary Feeds
fee.explanation: This fee allows a user to obtain preliminary quarterly inpatient or ambulatory surgery or emergency department encounter limited data set. It contains 16 out of the 18 safe harbor identifiers related to information about patient, diagnoses, provider, payers, charges and more. These data sets require review and approval by both an Institutional Review Board and the Health Data Committee prior to distribution of data to the user.
Database for agreements conducted under Federal government entities (HCUP). This data set includes healthcare facilities data.

category.feedisplay: Other Data Series and Licenses (Fee Discounts Apply)

fee.feedisplay: Other Data Series and Licenses - Fee Discounts
fee.explanation: Notes: The following discounts apply: Non-Contributing Carrier (50% for CAHPS Data Set); Contributing Carrier (75% for CAHPS Data Set); Prior Year (20% for HEDIS CAHPS Data Set); Years before Current and Prior Year (35% for HEDIS CAHPS Data Set); Student (75% for HEDIS CAHPS Data Set or Survey Responses); University or Not for Profit Entity (35% for HEDIS CAHPS Data Set or Survey Responses).

This fee is for a multi-use, multi-user license to access all data series included within this fee schedule. The license covers use for a 12-month period starting with the data specified in the licensing agreement and ending 365 days later. A 15% discount will be given if the license is renewed prior to the license termination date.
1 year multi-user license to access one data series. 15% discount for on-time renewals

fee.feedisplay: Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set Data Set
fee.explanation: This fee is for the current year of the standardized limited data set HEDIS indicators for Medicaid, Commercial, and CHIP health plans in Utah. HEDIS is a report of the quality of care provided by a plan to its enrollees.

fee.feedisplay: Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems Data Set
fee.explanation: This fee is for the current year of the Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems (CAHPS) Standardized limited data set. CAHPS measures enrollees' satisfaction with their health plan. Data are available for Medicaid, Commercial and CHIP health plans.

fee.feedisplay: Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems Survey Responses
fee.explanation: This fee is for the current year of the Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems (CAHPS) survey responses data set. CAHPS measures enrollees' satisfaction with their health plan. Data are available for Medicaid, Commercial and CHIP health plans.
Fee covers the cost to review the death record, collect signatures, and create the permit.

Fee covers the cost to review the death record and enter information from the record to the permit. Collect family signatures, funeral home signatures and cemetery signatures, review for accuracy, register the permit. Make a copy of the permit and file. This also funds a portion of the data applications used for this system.

This hourly fee applies to all custom work, including but not limited to: data extraction analytics; aggregate patient-risk profiles for clinics, payers or systems; data management reprocessing; data matching; and creation of samples or subsets.

fee.feedisplay: Additional Fields to create a custom data set
fee.explanation: This fee is for providing additional custom fields to a data set

fee.feedisplay: Convenience Fee (for Credit or Debit Card payment)

fee.explanation: This fee covers the cost to amend a record in our system.

Provides a way for customers ordering vital record certificates to prove entitlement as required in 26B-8-125(3)(a) if their identity cannot be verified through the Utah Drivers License or Utah State ID Card.

fee.feedisplay: Birth Parent Information Adoption Registry Registration
Death Notification Subscription Fee (organization less than or equal to 100,000 lives)
Death Notification Subscription Fee (organizations greater than 100,000 lives)
Online Access to Computerized Vital Records
fee.explanation: This is a fee other state agencies pay (per user) to have access to vital records online to complete business.
This fee is sent to Tyler Technologies and covers the cost of an online order for vital records certificates. It passes the cost of the online ordering through to the customer.

Provides a way for customers ordering vital record certificates to prove entitlement as required in 26B-8-125(3)(a) if their identity cannot be verified through the Utah Drivers License or Utah State ID Card.
This fee is charged to expedite processing a request/order.

fee.feedisplay: Handling of requested samples for shipping to outside lab.
fee.explanation: Processing of Office of the Medical Examiner samples for non-Office of the Medical Examiner testing.

fee.feedisplay: Handling and storage of requested samples by outside sources
fee.explanation: Storage fee (outside normal Office of the Medical Examiner retention schedule).

fee.feedisplay: Return request by immediate relative as defined in code UCA 26B-8-201(3)
fee.explanation: Sample return fee

fee.feedisplay: Daily charge for use of Medical Examiner Storage Facilities
fee.explanation: Beginning 24 hours after notification that body is ready for release.
$10.00 per permit payable to Vital Records for processing.
plus cost of body transportation
plus cost of body transportation

category.feedisplay: Expert Services - Forensic Pathologist Case Review, Consultation, and Testimony, Portal to Portal, up to 8 Hours/day

fee.feedisplay: Non-jurisdictional criminal and all civil cases

category.feedisplay: Use of Office of the Medical Examiner facilities by Non-Office of the Medical Examiner Pathologists

fee.feedisplay: Use of facilities and staff for autopsy
Use of facilities and staff for external examinations
Glass Slides (re-cuts, routine stains) per slide
Glass slides - Immunohistochemical stains per slide
Digital X-ray images from Digital Source (Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine).

fee.explanation: Digital photographic images.

fee.feedisplay: Digital Photos - Copies for immediate relative or legal representative as outlined in UCA 26B-8-217(2)(a)(i)-(ii)

fee.explanation: No charge for copies for law enforcement, physicians, attorneys and government entities as outlined in UCA 26B-8-217(2)(a)(iii)-(iv), and 26B-8-217(2)(b)(i)-(iv).

category.feedisplay: Copy of Autopsy and Toxicology Report

fee.explanation: No charge for copies for (1) immediate relative or legal representative as outlined in UCA 26B-8-217(2)(a)(i)-(ii) and (2) for law enforcement, physicians, attorneys and government entities as outlined in UCA 26B-8-217(2)(a)(iii)-(iv), and 26B-8-217(2)(b)(i)-(iv).

category.feedisplay: Copy of Miscellaneous Office of the Medical Examiner Case File Papers

fee.feedisplay: Copies for immediate relative or legal representative as outlined in UCA 26B-8-217(2)(a)(i)-(ii)
Case File Copies - All Other Requestors
fee.explanation: No charge for copies for law enforcement, physicians, attorneys and government entities as outlined in UCA 26B-8-217(2)(a)(iii)-(iv), and 26B-8-217(2)(b)(i)-(iv).

category.feedisplay: Use of Tissue Harvest Room for Acquisition

fee.feedisplay: Use of Tissue Harvest Room for Acquisition - Skin Graft
Use of Tissue Harvest Room for Acquisition - Bone
Use of Tissue Harvest Room for Acquisition - Heart Valve
Use of Tissue Harvest Room for Acquisition - Saphenous Vein
Use of Tissue Harvest Room for Acquisition - Eye

fee.explanation: Screening test to determine if digestion is required.

fee.feedisplay: Total Microcystins and Nodularins by ELISA
Norovirus detection by Polymerase Chain Reaction
Environmental Protection Agency 533 - Per-and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances
Measles Virus Detection by Polymerase Chain Reaction
Respiratory Syncytial Virus-Flu-COVID detection by PCR
fee.explanation: Test to detect RSV, Influenza A and B, and SARS-CoV-2 virus from one swab.

fee.feedisplay: Hepatitis B Virus Core Total Antibody
fee.explanation: Test to detect the presence of antibodies to the hepatitis B virus (HBV) core antigen in the blood. The result of this test will indicate if a person has been infected with Hepatitis B.

fee.feedisplay: Mycoplasma Genitalium Detection by Nucleic Acid Testing
fee.explanation: This fee covers testing costs in assessing if an individual has a Mycoplasma genitalium infection. Mycoplasma genitalium is a sexually transmitted organism affecting the health of both men and women and screening for it is vital for good health.

fee.feedisplay: Laboratory Testing of Public Health Significance
fee.explanation: The emergence of diseases and subsequent testing methods are unpredictable. This fee allows Utah Public Health Laboratory to offer a test that is vital to protecting the public as the need arises to help diagnosis and prevent illness.
Lab test for chloride in water sources.
Lab test to identify fluoride in water sources
This fee covers the cost for the analysis of Nitrate + Nitrite found in samples. This is a major component of Hazardous Algae Blooms.

fee.feedisplay: Environmental Protection Agency 200.8 Copper and Lead

fee.explanation: This fee covers the cost for the analysis of Nitrate + Nitrite found in samples. This is a major component of Hazardous Algae Blooms.
This fee covers the cost for the analysis of Nitrate + Nitrite found in samples. This is a major component of Hazardous Algae Blooms.
May include a digestion fee
Requires Calcium Magnesium tests

fee.feedisplay: Chlorophyll-A Corrected for Pheophytin A Environmental Protection Agency 445, 446 or equivalent

fee.explanation: Liter of water

fee.feedisplay: Standard Method 5210B Carbonaceous Biochemical/Soluble Oxygen Demand

category.feedisplay: Inorganic Surface Water (Lakes, Rivers, Streams) Tests

fee.explanation: This fee covers the cost of analyzing and preparing total phosphorus found in samples. This is a major component of Hazardous Algae Blooms.

category.feedisplay: Water Microbiology (Drinking Water and Surface Water)

fee.explanation: Colilert/Colisure

fee.feedisplay: Heterotrophic Plate Count by 9215 B Pour Plate
Primary Method Addition for Recognition Laboratories
Application Fee for Primary Out of State Laboratories
fee.explanation: Plus reimbursement of all travel expenses

fee.feedisplay: Application for National Environmental Accreditation Program Recognition

fee.explanation: This fee covers testing costs in assessing if an individual has an HIV infection. HIV detection in the community is a high priority in public health
This fee covers testing costs in assessing if an individual has a hepatitis infection.

fee.feedisplay: Match on Immunization Records in Database - 100,001-200,000 records
fee.explanation: This fee covers the immunization record matching service provided to health plans for Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set reporting. The service includes matching for up to 200,000 records per fiscal year. Querying and matching for clinical purposes, such as patient treatment, are not subject to a fee.

fee.feedisplay: Match on Immunization Records in Database - unlimited records
fee.explanation: This fee covers the immunization record matching service provided to health plans for Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set reporting. The service includes matching for unlimited records per fiscal year. Querying and matching for clinical purposes, such as patient treatment, are not subject to a fee.

fee.feedisplay: Match on Immunization Records in Database - unlimited records + customizations
fee.explanation: This fee covers the immunization record matching service provided to health plans for Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set reporting. The service includes matching for unlimited records per fiscal year plus additional customizations for the health plans, including but not limited to custom Virtual Private Networks, custom Admission, Discharge, and Transfer data feeds, and other extenuating circumstances. Querying and matching for clinical purposes, such as patient treatment, are not subject to a fee.

fee.feedisplay: Match on Immunization Records in Database - up to 100,000 records
fee.explanation: This fee covers the immunization record matching service provided to health plans for Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set reporting. The service includes matching for up to 100,000 records per fiscal year. Querying and matching for clinical purposes, such as patient treatment, are not subject to a fee.

fee.feedisplay: Match on Immunization Records in Database
fee.explanation: This fee covers the per record immunization record matching service provided to health plans for Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set reporting. Querying and matching for clinical purposes, such as patient treatment, are not subject to a fee.

Fees for approved special uses will be based on the fair market value of the use, determined by customary practices which may include: an assessment of comparable values for similar properties, comparable fees for similar land uses, or fee schedules. If more than one fee determination applies, the highest fee will be selected.

subsection.amend: Except as provided in Subsection (2), this bill takes effect July 1, 2026.
The actions affecting Section 1 (Effective upon governor's approval) take effect:
except as provided in Subsection (2)(b), May 6, 2026; or
if approved by two-thirds of all members elected to each house:
without the governor's signature, the day following the constitutional time limit of Utah Constitution, Article VII, Section 8; or
in the case of a veto, the date of veto override.
